name: read-arxiv-paper
tagline: Downloads an arxiv paper's LaTeX source, reads it, and writes a summary tying it back to the nanochat codebase.
verdict: Compact and mostly deterministic — skips re-downloading cached sources and checks for tag collisions before writing. But the summary's output filename is chosen by free-form agent judgment (non-reproducible across runs), download/entrypoint failures are unaddressed, and the terse description doesn't disclose the two persistent file writes it performs.

highlights.summary: Description says only 'read an arxiv paper'; body persists a tar.gz to ~/.cache and writes a summary file to ./knowledge, undisclosed in the description.
No exclusions or negative-trigger guidance stated anywhere.
"generate some reasonable tag ... Probably make sure that the tag doesn't exist yet" is vague at a load-bearing dedup step.
